references.name: unified_chrome_bar
references.description: All panels (interface, center, right) use the same chrome bar convention built by buildChromeBar(). Non-collapsible part: 'Actual Page' permalink link to production. Collapsible part (toggle arrow, collapsed by default): full metadata block — pub_id, title with version badge, tagline/description, pub_meta, pub_date, source_url, generated timestamp, citation. Replaces old doc-header and embed-topbar. State persisted in localStorage per panel. Front matter fields: pub_id, pub_meta, pub_version, pub_date, header_title, tagline, source_url, citation, permalink, permalink_fr.

references.name: toc_two_columns
references.description: Table of contents uses CSS two-column layout when there are 8 or more heading entries. Uses CSS columns property with break-inside:avoid on list items. Threshold: 8+ entries triggers toc-twocol class.

references.name: interface_panel_routing
references.description: When viewer runs embedded in content-frame (right panel of main navigator): interface links route to center-frame instead of loading in-place, main navigator link reloads the full page. External links (different origin) always open in a new browser tab. Routing is handled in the viewer's interceptInternalLinks using window.name detection. The navigator's srcdoc iframe has null origin so cross-document access from navigator to panel content is not possible.

references.name: soft_coupled_baseurl
references.description: Viewer auto-detects SITE.baseurl from window.location.pathname at runtime. Production baseurl (/knowledge) is kept as fallback for resolving Liquid tags in imported markdown. resolveInternalPath and viewerRewriteUrl both check viewer baseurl first, then production baseurl. resolveLiquid handles both single and double-quoted Liquid syntax.

references.name: doc_header_convention
references.description: Doc-header bar displays: toggle arrow (left), nav-back link (for /full/ pages back to summary), actual page permalink, version dot+tag. Collapsible section shows labeled rows: Publication (version · date · title), Generated (timestamp), Authors (default: Martin Paquet & Claude). Built by shared buildDocHeaderParts(meta) used in both standalone doc-header and embed chrome bar. Authors split by & displayed on separate lines.

references.name: export_corporate_styling
references.description: PDF and DOCX exports use corporate styling, not web theme colors. White background (#fff), black text (#111), corporate blue accent (#1d4ed8). Cover page centered horizontally with title, description, separator line, pub_meta, timestamp, authors. TOC on page 2 with page-break-after. Running header: title with blue bottom liner. Running footer: Generated timestamp · version, K_DOCS right-aligned, blue top liner. First page (cover) has empty header/footer. Filename: PUB_ID - Title - VER.pdf/.docx.

references.name: draggable_panel_dividers
references.description: Main navigator vertical dividers support both drag (mousedown/mousemove/mouseup with overlay) and click-to-step fallback. Left divider cycles [0, 220, 320]px. Right divider cycles 0 → 50% → full → 0. Grid uses pixel-based grid-template-columns with transition disabled during drag. Dividers 14px wide on desktop, 8px on mobile (≤768px) via CSS media query + JS DIVIDER_W. Styled with 5 grip dots, no arrows.

references.name: bilingual_en_fr
references.description: All documentation must be created in both English and French — always. EN files at docs/publications/<slug>/ and FR files at docs/fr/publications/<slug>/. EN front matter includes permalink_fr, FR front matter includes permalink_en. Same structure, same front matter fields, translated content. This applies to publications, success stories, interfaces, and any new documentation page. Never create EN without FR or vice versa.

references.name: live_mindmap_mindelixir
references.description: Live mindmap uses MindElixir v5.9.3 (replaces custom mermaid pan/zoom/click). Three deployment points from same data source: I5 standalone interface, K2.0 inline embed, viewer webcard. Mermaid-to-MindElixir converter strips node syntax and builds {topic, id, children} tree. Depth filtering via depth_config.json (JS port of mindmap_filter.py). Four themes synced with viewer via BroadcastChannel (kdocs-theme-sync): cayman, midnight, daltonism-light, daltonism-dark. Viewer is theme master — broadcasts to I5 iframe. I5 can also pick independently. MindElixir init: editable:false, contextMenu:false, keypress:false. Read-only: no node drag, no text editing, scroll zoom only. +/- expand/collapse handled natively by MindElixir. Full mode shows all branches (incl. architecture/constraints) at default depth via collapseDeep on init. Auto-fit on fullscreen toggle and window resize. I5 has bilingual help panel (? button, right sliding, localStorage persisted).

references.name: interface_padding_convention
references.description: Interface chrome bars (controls, toolbars) must live inside the same padded container as the content so they inherit identical horizontal padding (1rem). No separate padding values on bars. Does not apply to main navigator which has its own grid layout. All interfaces except main navigator follow this rule.

references.name: discrete_scrollbars
references.description: All interfaces and viewer pages use thin discrete scrollbars: 6px wide, transparent track, --border color for thumb, --muted on hover. Both Firefox (scrollbar-width: thin; scrollbar-color: var(--border) transparent) and Webkit (::-webkit-scrollbar 6px) covered. Applied via *, *::before, *::after selector.

references.name: bilingual_single_codebase
references.description: HARD RULE: All interfaces and web programs MUST have identical {::nomarkdown} body in both EN and FR index.md files. Only YAML front matter, h1 title, and cross-references section may differ. JS auto-detects language from document.documentElement.lang (set by viewer srcdoc) or URL path indexOf('/fr/'). All visible text translated at runtime via L.en/L.fr objects and translateStatic() function. No hardcoded language anywhere — never var LANG='fr'. When editing any interface, always verify both EN and FR bodies remain identical. This applies to all web pages: interfaces, publications, any program with language variants.

references.name: animated_webcard_pipeline
references.description: Animated mindmap webcards use a 2-step pipeline: (1) capture_mindmap.js renders MindElixir v5.9.3 in headless Puppeteer, capturing progressive PNG frames at 1200x630. (2) stitch_webcard.py adds KNOWLEDGE header/footer bars and stitches into animated GIF (256 colors, Floyd-Steinberg, MEDIANCUT). Cinematic 3-movie sequence: Emergence (root→overview), Collapse (retract to skeleton), Exploration (each branch opens progressively then closes). Normal mode: depth 2/3. Full mode (--full): depth 3/5, includes architecture+constraints. Timing: 800ms per frame (cinematic pace), 3000ms hold on final frame. 4 theme palettes: daltonism-light, daltonism-dark, cayman, midnight. Output: docs/assets/og/<slug>-<lang>-<theme>.gif. Typical: 121 frames, ~2MB, under 50MB social limit.

external_files.description: Visual documentation convention — video evidence extraction with OpenCV, contact sheets, deduplication
Interactive work sessions convention — resilient multi-delivery sessions with 5 persistence channels
Interactive conception convention — ideation, prototyping, progressive crystallization
Interactive diagnostic convention — structured hypothesis elimination with real-time issue documentation
Webcard generation convention — animated OG GIFs, 4 themes, 8-frame sequence, Pillow pipeline
OG social images + LinkedIn banners — static PNG and animated GIF social preview generation
Web page rendering convention — Playwright + Chromium headless for screenshots and Mermaid SVG
Validation protocol convention — QA checklist for interfaces, data pipelines, publications, modules
Interface CSS theme convention — wrapper variables, dark theme selectors, fallback values, no variable redefinition
